description: A lone bandit blocks the cart-track. Defeat him and you've earned your first scar in the Reach.
objective: Defeat the bandit blocking the road.
customIntroduction: The cart-track runs straight as far as you can see. A figure is sitting on a rock at the far end of the road, watching you come — and now he stands, drawing a blade. There is nowhere on this road to be that is not in his line of sight. Whatever happens next is going to be brief.
customContext: SETTING CUE: This is the player's first real encounter in the Reach — a tutorial fight against a single mundane road-bandit on an empty stretch of cart-track far from any Bureau patrol. ROLE: introduce the combat loop. ONE BANDIT only, spawned hostile (zone 4) at the eastern end of the path. The player starts at the western end (zone 1). RUN A SIMPLE FIGHT — do not narrate elaborate dialogue, do not have the bandit parley, do not spawn more enemies. The bandit attacks on his turn; the player learns ATTACK, MOVE, END TURN. If the player tries to speak to the bandit, he answers with a single hostile line and then attacks anyway. If the player tries to flee off the west edge, narrate them escaping but leave the encounter unresolved — the level-up award only fires when the bandit is killed. ON KILL: an automatic trigger awards 300 XP — the player will see a LEVEL UP button appear in the Player Panel. Narrate the moment briefly (the bandit drops; the road is quiet) and stop. Do NOT call award_xp yourself — the trigger handles it.
